_meta.unit: Early Task 3 — Hermès lexicon: leathers + permanent color core + recent seasonal colors

types.material: leather/skin name with grain+behaviour identity and production status
types.color: official color name; permanent-core, staple-recurring, or seasonal

_meta.caveat: Hermès permanent-vs-seasonal is fuzzy; colors drop out for years and return. Rows that cannot be cleanly classified say 'permanent-or-recurring, unverified' with lowered confidence. Official numeric color codes are the anchor (high); where the code lives only in a swatch image it is null and queued. Exotic leather blind-stamp symbols are from the established authentication beat, not re-confirmed vs auction catalog this run (medium).

name: Togo
note: Fine pebbled grain, female baby calf (Clemence half-sister), scratch-resistant, light. Current core.

name: Clemence
note: Large flat soft grain, male bull calf, semi-matte, heavier, slouchy. Created for luggage; ~1992 (Sellier: 1980s). Current core.

name: Epsom
note: Heat-embossed fine even grain, rigid, scratch-resistant, holds shape. Replaced Courchevel 2004. Current core.

name: Swift
note: Soft matte near-smooth, takes bright color, scratches easily. Gulliver discontinued 1999; Swift introduced 2004/2005. Current core.

name: Box
note: Oldest Hermès leather (named for shoemaker Joseph Box). Smooth glossy rigid, scratches then patinates to mirror sheen. Heritage, still produced.

name: Chevre Mysore
note: Goat; small bright grain, durable, scratch-resistant; most common goat leather; replaced Chèvre de Coromandel. Current.

name: Chevre de Coromandel
note: Goat; larger grain and more pronounced spine than Mysore; predecessor. Discontinued, replaced by Mysore.

name: Chevre Chamkila
note: Goat; firm, small smooth very shiny grain ('chamkila'='shiny' in Hindi). First produced 2018.

name: Barenia
note: Smooth calfskin, slight sheen, water/scratch resistant (oil-absorbing). Originally saddles; first on bags 1970s. Develops patina, darkens. Heritage, sought-after.

name: Barenia Faubourg
note: 'Reinterpretation of Barenia calfskin'; patinas/darkens, more supple than Barenia. Introduced 2016.

name: Evercolor
note: Soft near-smooth, tiny printed grain, slight sheen, softens over time. Introduced 2012. Current/intermittent.

name: Evergrain
note: Evercalf with tiny printed grain; soft, prone to scratches, becomes shiny/softer. Introduced 2004.

name: Fjord
note: Large-grained, sturdy, water-resistant; heavier/more structured than Togo; 1990s-era. Largely retired/rare. Identity from beat + guide list.

name: Courchevel
note: Stamped fine grain (Epsom predecessor look); grain wears at contact points, not refurbishable. Replaced by Epsom 2004.

name: Ardennes
note: Grained sturdy matte calf; 1990s-2000s workhorse. Long discontinued; Galop d'Hermès cited as its successor look.

name: Vache Liegee
note: Cowhide, deep rounded pronounced grain, structured. Rare/intermittent. Identity from established beat + guide list.

name: Vache Country
note: Soft large-grained cowhide, more structured/heavier than Negonda; mainly Garden Party. Introduced 2014.

name: Negonda
note: Large-grained, soft, matte, water-resistant, durable; mainly Garden Party. First produced 2007.

name: Novillo
note: Supple, very small grain, can resemble Togo/Evercolor but less structured; takes color well, develops sheen. Introduced 2015.

name: Maurice
note: Grained bull calf, substantial; intermittent production.

name: Sombrero
note: Smooth-to-fine-grain, supple yet structured matte calf. Intermittent/current. Identity from beat + guide list.

name: Tadelakt
note: Smooth soft but structured (Box-like), can look faintly mottled, may blister when wet; showcases color. Introduced 2007. Heritage, still produced.

name: Doblis
note: Hermès' suede; very soft, cannot get wet, shines where handled. First on bags 1930s. Heritage/intermittent.

name: Chamonix
note: Smooth matte calf similar to Box but without the high gloss. Heritage. Identity from beat + guide list.

name: Vache Naturel
note: Untreated smooth leather, very delicate, stains/scratches/water-spots, darkens and patinas. Heritage, returned to production.

name: Galop d'Hermes
note: Lightweight matte rigid, flat variable grain (Ardennes-like); few neutral colors (Noir, Indigo, Fauve, Ebene). Recent.

name: Ostrich
note: Quill-bump grain, takes color richly; cycles in/out (removed from SO charts ~2025 but still in regular production). Exotic.

name: Niloticus Crocodile
note: Nile croc; smaller more uniform/rectangular scales than Porosus; matte or shiny (lisse); blind-stamp symbol two-dots (∷). Symbol from established beat.

name: Porosus Crocodile
note: Saltwater croc; smallest most prized scales; sourced Australia; blind-stamp symbol caret (^). Symbol from established beat.

name: Alligator
note: US-sourced; larger squarer scales with visible umbilical line; matte or shiny; blind-stamp symbol square. Symbol from established beat.

name: Lizard
note: Tiny fine scales, high natural shine; cyclical (rested then returned). Exotic.

name: Noir
note: Deep true black. Permanent-core, one of the original five shades.

name: Gold
note: Warm camel-brown with golden/caramel undertone; iconic Birkin neutral. Permanent-core.

name: Rouge H
note: Deep dark red with brown undertone, near-burgundy ('Rouge Hermès'). Introduced 1925. Permanent-core, original five.

name: Etoupe
note: Earthy grey-brown taupe; bestselling everyday neutral. Permanent-core, original five.

name: Orange H
note: Hermès' signature box orange; no direct Pantone equivalent. Permanent-core, original five; harder to find lately.

name: Blanc
note: True white. Staple-recurring; harder to find lately.

name: Etain
note: Mid-dark cool pewter grey. Staple-recurring.

name: Rouge Casaque
note: Vibrant cool blue-based red with slight pink undertone. Staple-recurring.

name: Craie
note: Soft off-white/chalk with warm undertone; creamier than pure white. Staple-recurring.

name: Bleu Nuit
note: Deep night blue. Staple-recurring.

name: Gris Tourterelle
note: Soft taupe-grey, warm undertone ('turtledove'); perennial collector neutral. Code queued (in swatch image).

name: Gris Meyer
note: Warm velvety grey with brown undertone. Code queued.

name: Gris Asphalte
note: Cool dark slate grey. Permanent-or-recurring, unverified. From established beat; code queued.

name: Rouge Sellier
note: Deep red-brown-purple blend (saddle red). Code queued.

name: Bleu Saphir
note: Deep sapphire blue. Permanent-or-recurring, unverified. Code queued.

name: Bleu de Malte
note: Dark teal-leaning navy. Named as an anchor color in PurseBop 2025. Code queued.

name: Nata
note: Warm cream ('nata'=cream in PT/ES); near-white neutral. Code queued.

name: Beton
note: Cool light grey-beige stone neutral. Code queued.

name: Vert Cypres
note: Deep forest green. Named as anchor color in PurseBop 2025. Code queued.

name: Rose Confetti
note: Warm clear pink with peach undertone. Introduced 2014. Code queued.

name: Gris Perle
note: Light pearl grey, creamy undertone. Code queued.

name: Rouge Radieux
note: Cool blue-based bright lipstick red, similar to Rouge Vif. New SS25. Cross-checked PurseBop 2025.

name: Vert Mangrove
note: Dark muted teal-green with grey undertone (between Vert Cyprès and Bleu Orage). New SS25. Cross-checked PurseBop.

name: Bleu Tie
note: Muted mid-tone blue with grey hint (between Bleu de Malte and Bleu de Presse). New SS25. Cross-checked PurseBop.

name: Gris Pantin
note: Light icy blue-toned grey (near Gris Platine, Bleu Glacier). New SS25. Cross-checked PurseBop.

name: Gris Argente
note: Metallic silver; first regular-production metallic alongside Doré. New SS25. Cross-checked PurseBop.

name: New Bleu Hydra
note: Vivid electric blue; revival of the 2012 Bleu Hydra. Returning.

name: New Bleu Jean
note: Denim blue with grey undertone; reissue of the iconic Bleu Jean. Returning.

name: Ardoise
note: Cool blue-toned slate grey ('slate'); popular ~2010, faded, reappeared late 2024 into 2025. Returning.

name: Poussiere
note: Muted caramel-tinged beige ('dust'); near Trench/Parchemin. Returning. Cross-checked PurseBlog SS25.

name: Rose Darling
note: Pale pink; reached regular bag production late AW24 into SS25. New AW24.

name: Dore
note: Metallic gold; revealed around Diwali 2024. New regular-production metallic. Cross-checked PurseBop.

name: Gris Platine
note: Cool-toned platinum grey. Introduced 2024 (named in PurseBop 2025 as a 2024 intro).

name: Amethyste
note: Balanced purple, exotic leathers only. Returning old-school color in SS25.

name: Vert Fonce
note: Deep year-round green with contrast stitch. Returning in SS25.

name: Colvert; Ecorce; Prunoir; Rouge Garance; Terre Cuite; Toundra; Vert Maquis; Vert Veronese
note: Long-retired colors back in regular production SS25 (Terre Cuite noted on Ostrich). Returning. Logged as a group; split into rows when each is individually re-sourced.

name: Electrum hardware
note: NOT a leather or color: mixed palladium+gold hardware finish, debuted SS2025 on small Sellier Epsom styles. Logged for completeness.

appends: (A) staple-neutral codes filled via Lilac Blue chart cross-checked with Bag Religion + Christie's — these supersede the earlier null-code rows for the same color, do not delete the originals; (B) year-by-year seasonal-color backfill 2013-2023 from PurseBop annual guides, Christie's auction collecting guide (debut year + code), and The Birkin's & Kelly's House per-color season guide; (C) code fills for the existing 2024-2025 seasonal rows.
rigor: Reference-site debut years are medium unless Christie's (auction) or a 2nd source confirms (then high). Codes from the Lilac Blue chart are medium; double-confirmed codes are high. Year conflicts (Mauve Sylvestre, Vert Criquet, Vanille) flagged in-note, not smoothed. Codes that did not surface left null, never guessed.

name: Gris Tourterelle
note: CODE FILL (supersedes earlier null-code row). 81 (in Togo) per Lilac Blue, cross-checked Bag Religion (81). Soft taupe-grey 'turtledove'.

name: Gris Meyer
note: CODE+YEAR FILL. Introduced 2022 (PurseBop 2022 + Ginza Xiaoma). Code 0L (Togo) per Lilac Blue. Pure 'true' grey, minimal undertone.

name: Gris Asphalte
note: CODE FILL. M8 (in Togo) per Lilac Blue (single chart). Cool dark slate grey.

name: Rouge Sellier
note: CODE FILL. 0G (Togo/Epsom) per Lilac Blue. Saddle red-brown-purple. Birkin&Kelly dates the name to SS2021.

name: Bleu Saphir
note: CODE+YEAR FILL. 73 per Christie's + Lilac Blue; Christie's lot dated 2016. Deep sapphire blue, purple undertone in light, exotics-prized.

name: Bleu de Malte
note: CODE FILL. 7L (Clemence/Matte) per Lilac Blue. Dark teal-leaning navy.

name: Nata
note: CODE+YEAR FILL. I2 per Christie's ('first appeared AW2019') + Lilac Blue. Warm cream near-white, dark-brown oil edge.

name: Beton
note: CODE FILL. 8L per Christie's ('Glacier White', early Hermès white) + Lilac Blue + Bag Religion. Cool light grey-beige stone neutral. Christie's lot dated 2017.

name: Vert Cypres
note: CODE FILL. 60 per Lilac Blue. Deep forest green.

name: Rose Confetti
note: CODE+YEAR FILL. 1Q (Epsom) per Lilac Blue; Birkin&Kelly dates debut SS2013 (corrects earlier '2014' note). Warm clear peach-pink.

name: Gris Perle
note: CODE FILL. 80 (Togo/Clemence/Swift) per Lilac Blue (consistent across 3 leathers). Light pearl grey.

name: Gris Pale
note: CODE+YEAR FILL. M4 per Christie's ('released 2023') + Lilac Blue. Pale grey bordering white, flatter than Gris Perle.

name: Vert de Gris
note: CODE FILL. 6C (Clemence) per Lilac Blue. Grey-undertoned neutral green.

name: Kraft
note: NEW staple. 2H per Bag Religion + Lilac Blue. Kraft-paper tan; introduced 2013 (Birkin&Kelly).

name: Gris Mouette
note: NEW staple. 4Z (Togo) per Lilac Blue. Pure pale 'seagull' grey, a 2010s neutral icon (Privé Porter names it a 2010s 'It' grey).

name: Mushroom
note: NEW staple. 0T per Christie's ('released 2022') + Lilac Blue; PurseBop logs it FW2022. Washed-out cool grey bordering pale green.

name: Vanille
note: NEW staple. Y1 per Christie's. Creamy vanilla off-white shifting beige-to-light-khaki.

name: Ebene
note: NEW staple. Christie's code 46 — CONFLICTS with RareCollection's Rouge H 46 / Gold 06 (vs Christie's Gold 37). Different code conventions; flagged, NOT reconciled. Classic dark espresso brown.

name: Gris Ciment
note: NEW 2024 color. 0V per Christie's ('launched 2024'). Cement/'crystal' grey, low saturation. Cousin to Gris Misty.

name: Gris Misty
note: NEW 2024 color. 0H per Christie's ('addition to grey lineup in 2024'; Lilac Blue lists H0). Ethereal matte cool grey.

name: Dore
note: CODE FILL for existing Doré row. Y9 per Christie's ('released 2024'). Golden-glow metallic on goat leather.

name: Beige Marfa
note: New 2023 ('beige cargo'). Light sand beige near Trench. Code not surfaced; left null.

name: Sun
note: New 2023. Bright clean true yellow. Code not surfaced; left null.

name: New Bleu Jean
note: 2023 reissue of the iconic Bleu Jean (cleaner, deeper light blue). Note: PurseBop also lists 'New Bleu Jean' as a 2025 reissue — recurring.

name: Rose Pop
note: New 2023. True fuchsia/neon pink (echoes Pantone Viva Magenta). Code not surfaced; left null.

name: Yuka
note: New 2023. Grassy spring green. Code not surfaced; left null.

name: Vert Comics
note: Carried 2022 into 2023. Bright 'comic' green. 0Z per Lilac Blue.

name: Orange Minium
note: New SS2023. Bright neon-leaning mandarin orange (NOT box Orange H). 0X per Lilac Blue.

name: Limoncello
note: New 2023. Soft yellow with hint of green (between Jaune Poussin and Lime). 0Y per Lilac Blue.

name: Gris Neve
note: New 2023 (Christie's: 'introduced 2023'). Airy grey with cool green undertone, 'first-love' grey. 0W per Lilac Blue + Christie's.

name: New White
note: New 2023 (SO box). Fresh true white. 0U per Lilac Blue.

name: Bleu Zellige
note: New 2023 (single-site). Mediterranean tile-blue. Code not surfaced.

name: Vert Yucca
note: New 2023 (single-site). Yucca/agave green. Code not surfaced.

name: Mauve Pale
note: New FW2022. Soft muted pink, lighter sister to Mauve Sylvestre. 09 per Lilac Blue (Mauve Pale).

name: Vert Fizz
note: New FW2022. Pastel mint/pistachio green. Code not surfaced. Privé Porter flags it as a 2020s 'It' green.

name: Chai
note: New 2022 (Christie's: 'launched 2022'; PurseBop Feb 2022). Warm creamy masala-tea tan, near Quebracho/Biscuit. 0M per Christie's + Lilac Blue.

name: Cassis
note: New SS2022. Deep blackcurrant wine-purple (blend of Anemone + Raisin). Code not surfaced.

name: Vert Absinthe
note: New SS2022. Light yellow-green (lighter than 2021's Jaune Bourgeon). Code not surfaced.

name: Bleu Royale
note: New SS2022. Deep royal blue (near Bleu Electric). Code not surfaced.

name: Bleu Hydra
note: 2022 reissue (orig. 2012); reissued again 2025 ('New Bleu Hydra'). Most saturated electric blue. Recurring.

name: Aqua
note: 2022 reissue, chèvre-only (near Blue Atoll). Code not surfaced.

name: Rose Sakura
note: On the 2021 A La Carte Colorama (PurseBop Sept 2021). 3Q per Christie's + Lilac Blue. Soft cherry-blossom pink, the 'ultimate' Hermès pink, auction-prized.

name: Mauve Sylvestre
note: On the 2021 SO Colorama. X9 (Epsom) per Lilac Blue. Clear cool pink/mauve. YEAR CONFLICT: Birkin&Kelly dates it SS2019 — recurring, flagged.

name: Bleu Brume
note: On the 2021 SO Colorama. T0 (Epsom) per Lilac Blue. Pale misty grey-blue.

name: Jaune Poussin
note: On the 2021 SO Colorama. 1Z (Epsom) per Lilac Blue. Pale soft 'chick' yellow.

name: Vert Criquet
note: On the 2021 SO Colorama; Christie's + Privé Porter name it a defining green. 3I (Epsom) per Lilac Blue. Cheerful tennis-ball green. Some sources date it 2020 — recurring.

name: Jaune Bourgeon
note: PurseBop 2022 names it 'last year's' (=2021); Birkin&Kelly SS2021. Yellow-green 'bud' yellow. Code not surfaced.

name: Rose Shocking
note: SS2021 (single-site). Bright shocking pink. Code not surfaced.

name: Vert Jade
note: SS2021 (single-site). Soft jade green. Code not surfaced.

name: Bleu France
note: PurseBop 2022: 'suspect released in 2021'. Clean bright blue. Year approximate; code not surfaced.

name: Bleu Frida
note: FW2020 (single-site for year). 0F per Lilac Blue. Saturated mid-blue.

name: Rose d'Ete
note: SS2020 (single-site). Soft summer rose pink. Code not surfaced.

name: Jaune de Naples
note: SS2019 (single-site). Sun-kissed mid yellow. Code not surfaced.

name: Vert Amande
note: 2019 (single-site). Soft almond green. Code not surfaced.

name: Rouge de Coeur
note: 2019 (single-site). Vivid heart-red. Code not surfaced.

name: Bleu du Nord
note: SS2019 (single-site). Cool northern blue. Code not surfaced.

name: Jaune Ambre
note: SS2018 (Birkin&Kelly + Christie's lot dated 2018). 9D per Christie's. Warm amber-yellow with orange-brown undertone.

name: Rose Lipstick
note: SS2018 (single-site). Bright blue-based lipstick pink. Code not surfaced.

name: Toundra
note: 2018 debut (Birkin&Kelly); returned to regular production SS2025 (PurseBlog SS25). Warm 'tundra' brown. Code not surfaced.

name: Rouge Tomate
note: 2016 (single-site). Lively tomato red. Code not surfaced.

name: Rose Azalee
note: FW2015 (single-site; also named in madisonavenuecouture investing guide). Warm coral-pink azalea. Code not surfaced.

name: Orange Poppy
note: 2015 (single-site). Bright poppy orange. Code not surfaced.

name: Craie
note: YEAR FILL for the existing Craie staple row. Christie's: 'released in the AW2013 collection'. Soft chalk-white with light grey. Code 10 already logged.

name: Bleu Paradis
note: SS2014 (single-site). 2T per Lilac Blue. Bright tropical-water blue.

name: Bamboo
note: 2014 (single-site). Mid bamboo green. Code not surfaced.

name: Vert Titien
note: PRE-2015 LEAD (single-site, old year). Debut 2007 (homage to Titian). Logged low, not a fabricated season grid.

name: Potiron
note: PRE-2015 LEAD (single-site, old year). Debut 2003, pumpkin orange. 5F per Lilac Blue. Logged low.
